programme.title: SC v. Alex Murdaugh
programme.desc: SLED investigators map it all out using data from their phones during their deaths.

programme.title: SC v. Alex Murdaugh
programme.desc: Prosecutors said that technology placed Alex Murdaugh at the crime scene where Maggie and Paul were gunned down on the family's Moselle property.

programme.title: SC v. Alex Murdaugh
programme.sub-title: Critique of the State's Murder Timeline
programme.desc: The defense questions the SLED agent who put together a timeline of events.

programme.title: SC v. Alex Murdaugh
programme.desc: The SLED agent on the stand testified about the timeline put together that showed the movements of Alex, Maggie, and Paul Murdaugh the night of the murders.

programme.title: SC v. Alex Murdaugh
programme.desc: Alex Murdaugh's defense team begins its case; the strategy is to poke holes in the state's case.

programme.title: SC v. Alex Murdaugh
programme.desc: Alex Murdaugh's oldest and only surviving son Buster took the stand to try to help his father's case. Buster wasn't home when his mother, Maggie, and younger brother, Paul, were killed.

programme.title: Suitcase Murder Trial
programme.desc: In her second police interview Sarah Boone was more animated. The jury saw her on the video recording crying and screaming at detectives. Police wanted a confession out of her after they determined she intentionally kept her boyfriend zipped up in a suitcase for hours. This second interrogation happened on two days after Jorge Torres lost his life suffocating in a suitcase. Police declared his death a homicide.

programme.title: Suitcase Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors watched Sarah Boone deny any intention to the murder of her boyfriend in her second police interrogation. Detectives were in the beginning of their investigation trying to figure out what led to Jorge Torres being found dead in a suitcase. The first police interview happened at the crime scene. The next day after police did the initial part of their investigation that concluded it was a homicide and was seeking a confession out of her for her role in the murder.

programme.title: Suitcase Murder Trial
programme.desc: Cross-examination began on lead detective Chelsey Koepsell. It came after the jury listened and watched the police interrogation of the defendant two days after Jorge Torres suffocated to death inside a suitcase. Jurors learned of the police procedure in the early part of the murder investigation of Jorge Torres. Detective Koepsell recalled taking Sarah Boone's cellphone without her consent because there was incriminating evidence on it.

programme.title: Suitcase Murder Trial
programme.desc: Sarah Boone took the stand in her own murder trial. It came after the prosecution rested their case against her for intentionally killing Jorge Torres, her boyfriend, she was living with. Jurors listened to the defendant give them the backstory of how she met Torres and began their intimate relationship. Domestic violence was her defense. But she testified that during the very hours Torres was suffocating in the suitcase she simply had enough wine and fell asleep.

programme.title: Burn Pile Murder Trial
programme.desc: A key witness for the State gave more insight into the messy family life of the Farris'. Scott Farris explained his childhood years growing up and the day he came across the corpse of his father. Mountain Dews and cigarettes were Gary Farris' daily addictions before he died, according to both his sons who testified. Prosecutors wanted to make clear to jurors that health issues were not the culprit that led to his remains being found in a burn pile on his property.

programme.title: Burn Pile Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors learned about the lavish lifestyle of the Farris family. On the stand, Scott Farris, the youngest son of Gary and Melody, recalled how Melody liked to shop and had full access to the family account until months before the murder. The financial arrangement between her husband, Gary, and their youngest son was made clear to jurors. From the time they brought the 10-acre family farm, Scott was living and working as a farmhand.

programme.title: Burn Pile Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: The Son And The Family's Farmhand
programme.desc: On the stand, Scott Farris explained to the jury how his father, Gary, operated and managed the burn pile on the family farm. It was there where his charred remains were found. Later in their deliberations, the jury would have to weigh heavily Scott's alibi that July 4th holiday. He was living and working on the property where his father was killed and then later buried in this burn pile. The Defense wanted to raise any evidence that pointed to Scott to exonerate Melody Farris for malice murder.

programme.title: Burn Pile Murder Trial
programme.desc: It was the second day of the defendant's youngest son testifying against her. Scott Farris broke down details of those weeks leading up to his father, Gary's, shooting death. Scott emotionally recalled coming across his father's remains while the family was looking all around for him and immediately calling 911. He also recalled how his mother, the defendant, was faking her emotions when Gary's corpse was discovered.

programme.desc: In 2013, a newborn baby know as "Baby Precious" is found dead. DNA technology tracks down the child's parents.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.desc: "Deadly Desire" covers the extensive investigation into Jodine Serrin's horrific murder and how forensic genealogy finally cracked the case.

programme.desc: For two couples on vacation, an island paradise turned to hell when one couple murders the other. Eleven years after the murder, a drifter sailing the high seas to escape a drug charge was convicted of the crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1992, a young mother was gunned down in front of her children and police were left baffled. The crime seemed unmotivated and random. But family members hinted that the victim, who had been married to a prominent attorney, harbored a dark secret.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The Los Macheteros terrorist organization sought to end U.S. rule of Puerto Rico through violence. When the group claimed responsibility for an attack on 18 unarmed servicemen traveling near San Juan, the FBI is called in to investigate the killers.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On October 1, 1993, a stranger abducted 12-year-old Polly Klaas during a slumber party in her home. FBI agents collected fibers from her bedroom and discovered, through the use of laser light sources, a palm print on Polly's bed.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In October 1993, a robber surprised unsuspecting customers by dropping through the roof of a California bank.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1993, a mother, her 8-year-old son and a live-in nurse were brutally murdered by an assassin. The murder-for-hire plot was meticulously planned and executed.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The bodies of several young women began turning up in shallow graves in the Alaskan wilderness. In one of the first cases of successful criminal investigative analysis, FBI profilers mapped the criminal's mind and captured the serial killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1987, Cuban prisoners at the Atlanta Federal Prison broke out homemade weapons and stole from prison staff.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The decapitation of an African-American male led the Miami-Dade police and the FBI along a trail of murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.sub-title: The First and Kennedy Street Crew
programme.desc: Powerful street gangs in Washington D.C. enforced their power with deadly violence. The gang leader had fled to Africa, a hunt that ended in the 1994 Washington D.C. Metropolitan Police Headquarters shootout.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 1990s, a gang based in the U.S. Virgin Islands smuggled large amounts of cocaine into mainland America. Police and federal agents targeted the violent group, but its leader remained a mystery.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1976, a car bombing in Washington DC's Embassy Row claimed the lives of a former Chilean ambassador.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Two criminals, who met in prison while serving time for previous murders, struck up a friendship, which had deadly consequences.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Steven Dean Gordon and Frank Cano murdered at least four sex workers in California between 2012 and 2013.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: An investigation into the man known as the 'Babes in the Wood Killer,' who evaded justice for thirty years.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A family's worst nightmare when 16-year-old Becky Watts goes missing from her home and is found dead a few days' later, murdered by her own stepbrother.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Robert Hansen was a monster who preyed on the most vulnerable women in society - dehumanising them for his own twisted fantasies. Stranded with their killer in the vast Alaskan wilderness his victims had no chance of survival.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Gloria, a devoted mother, tried to stop her son David's forbidden relationship. Her attempt to protect her family ended in tragedy as David's hidden violent streak erupted with devastating consequences.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Victor Farrant is a convicted rapist who murdered his former girlfriend in 1996 and attempted to murder another woman after he was released from prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the Baton Rouge Serial Killer, known for murdering seven women in the area and sentenced to death for his chilling crimes.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of serial killer Geoffrey Evans and his accomplice John Shaw, who hit headlines in 1976 after the murders of two women in Ireland.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of a terrifying killer who snatched away the lives of four victims before being caught while trying to claim his fifth.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: New Jersey native Richard Kuklinski ran a burglary ring, distributed pirated pornography and dealt in weapons and drugs. He also claimed to have murdered over 100 people while working as a hit man for New York and New Jersey's crime families.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Two young mothers vanished from East London's streets. At first unconnected, the cases came together when evidence found in Derek's home showed both women suffered the same violent fate at his hands.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Arthur Shawcross, known as the 'Genesee River Killer,' was a serial killer who murdered eleven women.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Charles seemed like a helpful nurse, moving easily between hospitals. However, behind the façade, he had been secretly killed patients for years, avoiding suspicion by constantly changing jobs until investigators uncovered his deadly pattern.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: The story of Tyler Davis Bingham, pretty criminal turned leader of the brutal and feared Aryan Brotherhood.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Edward Johnson is sent to prison for the brutal murder of a female friend, but the violence doesn't end there.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A two-year-old boy was rushed to the hospital suffering from brain seizures and breathing difficulties.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mother claims that an intruder murdered her two sons, but lacking a motive, police use blood-spatter analysis and behavioral profiling to reconstruct a different scenario.

programme.desc: A 1995 fire razes the mansion of a Kansas doctor, killing two of her three children; disturbing accounts from an ex-husband lead investigators to believe the doctor set the blaze herself.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A husband confesses to his wife's murder, only to be found not guilty a year later when a phone tip and forensic evidence point to another.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a Pennsylvania man checks into a hospital for leg pain and later dies of thallium poisoning, police investigate his wife and find her in the process of collecting an insurance payment for her previous husband's death.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.desc: Police exhume the body of Karla Brown, murdered in her home in 1978, to study bite wounds, which eventually lead to the killer.

programme.desc: A beverage company pleads guilty to violating federal safety laws when a baby dies from E. coli after drinking apple juice.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the wife of a well-known dentist is found dead, police struggle to identify a suspect. Some fibers and a study of the weather patterns on the night of the murder break the case open.

programme.desc: When a decomposed body is found stuffed in a barrel, it's easy to presume murder. One of the few clues was an address book found along with the body.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.desc: Nancy Newman and her two daughters are found brutally murdered. The crime scene has no shortage of evidence.

programme.desc: Police charge a mentally unstable man with his wife's murder, but a medical examiner discovers that she died of natural causes.

programme.desc: Lucas's self-confidence begins to slip away when he misses an easy shot that could have cost Mark his life.
